DISCOVERY DEPT. 302: PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS NATIONAL BANK'S MOTION TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS UNOPPOSED AND GRANTED. THE MATTERS SPECIFIED IN PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, SET ONE ARE DEEMED ADMITTED. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $360.00 TO PAID WITHIN THIRTY DAYS FROM THE DATE OF SERVICE OF A COPY OF THIS ORDER WITH NOTICE OF ENTRY. A COPY OF THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS WHICH ARE THE SUBJECT OF THIS ORDER SHALL BE ATTACHED TO THE ORDER AS AN EXHIBIT. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT THE EMAIL ADDRESS PROVIDED IN THE TENTATIVE RULING. calendar.matters:
COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Dec-13-2021 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, been assigned by Department 206 for Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Seth Cameron Sunberg, a/k/a Seth Sunberg, an individual, and Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Prison Bars, Inc., a California corporation.
Dept: 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:35 a.m.

Henry Yandell, Esq. appeared for plaintiff American Express National Bank, Successor by Merger to American Express Bank, FSB.
Raquel Hernandez, Assistant Custodian of Records for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
Both Mr. Yandell and Ms. Hernandez appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendants Seth Cameron Sunberg, a/k/a Seth Sunberg, an individual, or Prison Bars, Inc., a California corporation

Plaintiff's Complaint for Common Counts {Open Book Account & Account Stated} and Quantum Meruit was filed on February 5, 2020.
Substitute service of plaintiff's Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents on defendants Seth Cameron Sunberg, a/k/a Seth Sunberg, an individual, and Prison Bars, Inc., a California corporation, occured on February 11, 2020, and was effectuated on February 21, 2020.
General Denial by defendant Seth Cameron Sunberg, a/k/a Seth Sunberg, an individual, was filed on March 10, 2020.
Order to Deem Matters Admitted and for Sanctions was filed on December 1, 2020.
Default as to defendant Prison Bars, Inc., a California corporation, was entered on March 1, 2021.
Notice of Time & Place of Trial was served on April 15, 2021, setting trial for November 1, 2021. Matter was assigned to Department 514 for a Short Cause Trial/Prove-Up hearing on November 15, 2021, and continued to today, December 13, 2021.
Plaintiff's Trial Brief, Exhibit & Witness List and Memo of Costs were filed on October 8, 2021.
DOE defendants dismissed today, December 13, 2021, by oral motion of counsel, but Request for Dismissal shall be e-filed with the Clerk's Office, forthwith.

Plaintiff American Express National Bank, Successor by Merger to American Express Bank, FSB, submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: Card Member Agreement......................................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 2: Copies of Monthly Credit Card Statements...................................ID/EVID

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff American Express National Bank, Successor by Merger to American Express Bank, FSB, and against defendant Seth Cameron Sunberg, a/k/a Seth Sunberg, an individual, and defaulted defendant Prison Bars, Inc., a California corporation, as follows:

Principal/Damages - $11,728.67 = Amount pled in the complaint.
Prejudgment Interest - Waived.
Attorney Fees - Waived.
Costs - $712.83.
Total - $12,441.50.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review signature.

Plaintiff STIPULATES to the return of the exhibits.

Proceedings concluded at 10:45 a.m.

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Nov-15-2021 Continued To Dec-13-21 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 514

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2021 Assigned To Trial Court

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-15-2021 Set For Trial On Nov-01-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-24-2021 Continued To May-05-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Matters Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-24-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance is made in this matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS NATIONAL BANK'S Motion To Deem Matters Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions is unopposed and GRANTED. The matters specified in plaintiff's Requests for Admissions, Set One are deemed admitted. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $360.00 to paid within thirty days from the date of service of a copy of this Order with Notice of Entry. A copy of the Requests for Admissions which are the subject of this Order shall be attached to the Order as an exhibit. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at the email address provided in the tentative ruling.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven B. Stein: Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: not reported (Disc302)
